questions.question: Explain the structure of struct sigaction and its members.
keyPoints: sa_handler: pointer to signal handler function
sa_sigaction: pointer to extended handler (when SA_SIGINFO set)
sa_mask: signals blocked during handler execution
sa_flags: modify signal handling behavior

questions.question: What are the advantages of sigaction() over the traditional signal() function?
keyPoints: More portable across different UNIX variants
Allows blocking specific signals during handler execution (sa_mask)
Provides SA_RESTART flag for automatic system call restart
Supports SA_SIGINFO for additional signal information (sender PID, UID)
More control over signal handling behavior

questions.question: Explain the purpose of the SA_RESTART flag with an example.
keyPoints: Automatically restarts system calls interrupted by signals
Prevents EINTR error from system calls like read(), write()
Useful for handling I/O operations without manually checking for interruptions
Not all system calls are restartable (e.g., select())

questions.question: Describe the siginfo_t structure and its important members.
keyPoints: si_signo: signal number that caused the signal
si_pid: process ID of the signal sender
si_uid: user ID of the signal sender
si_code: reason code for signal generation
si_status: exit status or signal number (for child signals)

questions.question: Write a C program to handle SIGINT (Ctrl+C) using sigaction().
keyPoints: Include <signal.h>
Define struct sigaction with sa_handler
Use sigemptyset() to initialize sa_mask
Set sa_flags appropriately (e.g., 0)
Call sigaction() with SIGINT and address of struct
Return 0 on success, -1 on error

questions.question: What is the purpose of sa_flags in sigaction()? List any four important flags.
keyPoints: Modifies behavior of signal handling
SA_RESTART: restart interrupted system calls
SA_SIGINFO: use extended handler with siginfo_t
SA_RESETHAND: reset handler after one use
SA_NODEFER: allow signal to interrupt its own handler
SA_NOCLDSTOP: don't generate SIGCHLD for stopped children

questions.question: Explain sigprocmask() and its three operations.
keyPoints: Used to examine/change process signal mask
SIG_BLOCK: add signals to current mask (block them)
SIG_UNBLOCK: remove signals from current mask (unblock)
SIG_SETMASK: replace current mask with new set
Useful for critical sections that must not be interrupted

questions.question: Write a complete C program that creates a signal handler for SIGALRM using sigaction() with SA_SIGINFO. The handler should display sender information.
keyPoints: Define sigaction structure
Initialize sa_sigaction (not sa_handler for SA_SIGINFO)
Initialize sa_mask using sigemptyset()
Set SA_SIGINFO in sa_flags
Setup siginfo_t parameter in handler
Display si_pid, si_uid, si_signo in handler
Use setitimer() or alarm() to generate SIGALRM
Proper error checking for sigaction() call

questions.question: Explain the difference between signal() and sigaction(). Also demonstrate how to implement a robust signal handler that blocks other signals during execution.
keyPoints: signal() is simple but not portable (behavior varies)
sigaction() is POSIX-compliant with more control
sigaction() allows sa_mask for blocking signals
sigaction() supports SA_RESTART for system calls
Show code with sa_mask set to block SIGINT during SIGALRM handling
Explain importance of signal blocking for critical handlers
Use sigaddset() to add signals to mask
Use sigemptyset() to initialize mask

questions.question: Design a signal handling scheme for a daemon process that: (a) Ignores SIGHUP, (b) Catches SIGTERM for graceful shutdown, (c) Catches SIGUSR1 to reload configuration, (d) Uses sigaction() with proper flags.
keyPoints: Use SA_IGNORE for ignoring SIGHUP (or set handler to SIG_IGN)
Implement SIGTERM handler with cleanup and exit
Implement SIGUSR1 handler to reload config
Set appropriate sa_flags (e.g., SA_RESTART for system calls)
Block other signals during critical handlers using sa_mask
Use sigemptyset() and sigaddset() for signal sets
Return appropriate error codes
Explain daemon initialization and cleanup

questions.question: Write a program that implements a timeout mechanism using alarm() and sigaction(). The program should attempt a long operation and abort if it takes more than 5 seconds.
keyPoints: Set up SIGALRM handler using sigaction()
Use SA_RESTART to prevent read/write interruption
Call alarm(5) to set 5-second timeout
Perform the long operation (e.g., reading from pipe/FIFO)
If alarm fires, handler executes and operation returns error
Cancel alarm with alarm(0) if operation completes
Handle EINTR error from interrupted system call
Include proper error checking
